## Getting Started

### 1. Set Up Your Environment

**Required software**:
```bash
# Install Python 3.8+
python --version

# Install dependencies
pip install -r requirements.txt

# Verify installation
python -c "import [key_library]; print([key_library].__version__)"
```

**Optional tools**:
- [Tool 1]: [Why it's helpful]
- [Tool 2]: [Why it's helpful]

**Detailed setup**: See [docs/setup.md](docs/setup.md)

### 2. Clone This Repository

```bash
git clone https://github.com/Philly-AI-Tech-Alliance/[repo-name].git
cd [repo-name]
```

### 3. Start Learning!

**Option A: Follow along linearly**
```bash
cd modules/01-[module-name]
open README.md
```

**Option B: Jump to specific topic**
- See [Table of Contents](#table-of-contents) below
- Navigate to relevant module

**Option C: Do exercises only**
```bash
cd exercises/
# Work through exercises in order
```

---

## How to Use This Content

### For Self-Paced Learners

**Recommended approach**:
1. Read module README for overview
2. Work through lessons in order
3. Complete hands-on exercises
4. Check solutions only after attempting
5. Complete module project
6. Take self-assessment quiz
7. Move to next module when confident

**Stuck?** See [Getting Help](#getting-help) section

### For Study Groups

**Using with a group**:
1. Set meeting schedule (weekly recommended)
2. Everyone completes module before meeting
3. Discuss challenges and insights
4. Work through exercises together
5. Present projects to each other
6. Celebrate progress!

**Find study groups**: [Philly AI Tech Alliance Slack](https://join.slack.com/philly-ai-tech) - #study-groups

### For Instructors/Facilitators

**Using for teaching**:
- All content is CC-BY-4.0 licensed (see [License](#license))
- Adapt to your context
- See `docs/facilitator-guide.md` for tips
- Slide decks available in `slides/` directories
- Facilitator notes included with exercises

**Using in academic setting?** We'd love to hear about it!

### For Workshops

**2-4 hour workshop format**:
1. Choose 1-2 modules
2. Focus on hands-on exercises
3. Use slide decks from `slides/`
4. Provide setup instructions in advance
5. Share repository for self-paced follow-up

## Getting Help

### Stuck on Content?

**Try this first**:
1. Re-read the relevant lesson
2. Check the glossary for term definitions
3. Review code comments carefully
4. Look at exercise hints
5. Compare with solution (if you tried first!)

**Still stuck?**

**Community support**:
- **Slack**: [#help](https://join.slack.com/philly-ai-tech) channel
- **GitHub Discussions**: [Ask a question](https://github.com/Philly-AI-Tech-Alliance/[repo-name]/discussions)
- **Office Hours**: Weekly Q&A sessions (check Slack for schedule)

**Found an error?**
- Open an [issue](https://github.com/Philly-AI-Tech-Alliance/[repo-name]/issues)
- Be specific: what's wrong, where, what you expected

### Technical Issues

**Environment setup problems**:
- Check `docs/setup.md` for detailed instructions
- Verify Python version: `python --version`
- Try recreating virtual environment
- Ask in #tech-support on Slack

**Code not working**:
- Check you're in correct directory
- Verify dependencies installed: `pip list`
- Look for typos in code
- Check against solution code

---

## Contributing

We welcome contributions from the community!

### Ways to Contribute

**Beginner-friendly**:
- Fix typos or broken links
- Improve explanations
- Add comments to code examples
- Suggest additional resources
- Report issues

**Intermediate**:
- Add new exercises
- Create visualizations or diagrams
- Write additional examples
- Improve documentation

**Advanced**:
- Create new modules
- Develop new projects
- Review pull requests
- Mentor learners

### How to Contribute

1. **Read the guide**: [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md)
2. **Check open issues**: [Issues](https://github.com/Philly-AI-Tech-Alliance/[repo-name]/issues)
3. **Open discussion**: Propose major changes first
4. **Submit PR**: Follow the PR template
5. **Get reviewed**: Respond to feedback
6. **Celebrate**: You helped make education better!

**First time contributing?** Look for issues labeled `good-first-issue`

## Frequently Asked Questions

### About the Content

**Q: Is this course really free?**
A: Yes! All content is open source. You may need cloud credits for some exercises (~$5-10).

**Q: How long does it take to complete?**
A: [X-Y] hours total. Most complete in [timeframe] at [pace].

**Q: Do I get a certificate?**
A: Currently: GitHub completion badge. Future: Digital certificates planned.

**Q: Can I use this for my class/company?**
A: Yes! Content is CC-BY-4.0 licensed. Just provide attribution.

**Q: Is this kept up to date?**
A: Yes, reviewed [annually/quarterly]. Last updated: [date]. Report outdated info via issues.

### Technical Questions

**Q: What programming language is used?**
A: [Language(s)]. [Specific version requirements].

**Q: Do I need a GPU?**
A: [Yes/No]. [Alternatives if needed].

**Q: What OS is supported?**
A: [Windows/Mac/Linux]. Detailed setup for each in docs/setup.md.

**Q: Can I do this on mobile/tablet?**
A: Possible but not recommended. Desktop/laptop strongly preferred for coding exercises.

## License

### Code Examples

All code in this repository is licensed under the **MIT License**.

See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for full text.

**TL;DR**: Use the code however you want, no attribution required (but appreciated!)

### Educational Content

All educational materials (text, slides, images, videos, etc.) are licensed under the **Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC-BY-4.0)**.

See [LICENSE-CONTENT](LICENSE-CONTENT) for full text.

**TL;DR**: You can:
- Use for personal or commercial purposes
- Modify and adapt
- Redistribute

**You must**:
- Provide attribution to original authors
- Link to license
- Indicate if you made changes

### Attribution

When using or adapting this content, please include:

```
Based on [Content Title] by [Author Name(s)] and Philly AI Tech Alliance
Source: https://github.com/Philly-AI-Tech-Alliance/[repo-name]
Licensed under CC-BY-4.0
```
